</imports>

<findings>

[observation] [warning] 11 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [info] Contains 60% of project files (87/146) — subdivided into 7 sub-zones
[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.9) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [warning] Bidirectional imports with both 'Static Analysis Services' (12 out / 5 in) and 'Branch Risk Domain' (8 each way) create zone-level cycles that obscure actual dependency direction — the shared dependencies should be pushed into a common foundation module.
[observation] [info] Entry points span schema, CLI, and analyzer layers simultaneously, which is appropriate for a package core but means consumers must navigate multiple entry surfaces — the public.ts barrel should remain the canonical single entry point.
[observation] [info] Zone has exemplary internal cohesion (0.9) and very low external coupling (0.1), indicating a well-bounded implementation layer.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "sourcevision-analysis-core" has files across 12 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] No index.ts or public barrel file exists at the zone boundary to make the external API surface explicit. All 87 files are directly importable by other zones, meaning zone membership is enforced only by community-detection metadata, not by code structure. Any new import added to any of the 87 files silently extends the zone boundary.
[suggestion] [critical] Zone imports from unit-cli (7 imports: domain consuming leaf/CLI layer) while also correctly importing from data-file-schema (foundation). This barbell profile — consuming both the foundation layer and the leaf layer simultaneously — means the zone has no stable tier position and cannot be isolated for independent versioning or replacement.
[pattern] [warning] sourcevision-analysis-core is the sole mandatory intermediary for all cross-zone traffic in the package — zero zones communicate with each other without passing through it, making it a single point of architectural failure for any zone-boundary refactor
[relationship] [critical] unit-cli participates in a three-node cycle: unit-2 → unit-cli (7 imports) and unit-cli → unit-2 (1 import) closes a direct cycle, while unit-cli → unit (1 import) and unit → unit-2 (8 imports) closes a second indirect cycle through unit — two overlapping cycles sharing one edge
[anti-pattern] [warning] The 'analyzers' sub-zone (8 files, cohesion 0.21) has the lowest cohesion of any named zone, indicating its 8 files do not form a natural cluster. Files grouped there likely share a directory path rather than import relationships — cohesion below 0.3 in an 8-file zone suggests artificial grouping that obscures actual dependencies.
[anti-pattern] [critical] Zone encompasses 87 files at cohesion 0.9, effectively absorbing the entire package into a single unit. Zone detection has failed to partition the codebase into actionable boundaries — the 7 internal sub-zones should be elevated to top-level zones so that cross-zone import analysis reflects real architectural intent rather than a single monolithic blob vs. a few satellite micro-zones.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.9) — files are tightly interconnected
- Contains 60% of project files (87/146) — subdivided into 7 sub-zones
- 11 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- With 87 files and cohesion of 0.9, this zone is the dominant core of the sourcevision package — consolidating analyzers, schema, CLI commands, and PDF export into a tightly coupled unit.
- The 12 outbound imports to 'Static Analysis Services' (analyzers) combined with 5 inbound returns suggest those 8 files may belong here rather than in a separate zone — Louvain likely over-partitioned.
- The bidirectional import relationship with 'Branch Risk Domain' (unit, 8 each way) is the key coupling concern to resolve — it indicates a shared-state or utility dependency that should be extracted into a lower-level module.
- Zone has exemplary internal cohesion (0.9) and very low external coupling (0.1), indicating a well-bounded implementation layer.
- Bidirectional imports with both 'Static Analysis Services' (12 out / 5 in) and 'Branch Risk Domain' (8 each way) create zone-level cycles that obscure actual dependency direction — the shared dependencies should be pushed into a common foundation module.
- Entry points span schema, CLI, and analyzer layers simultaneously, which is appropriate for a package core but means consumers must navigate multiple entry surfaces — the public.ts barrel should remain the canonical single entry point.
- Zone "sourcevision-analysis-core" has files across 12 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- unit-cli creates a three-node cycle with unit-2: unit-2 exports 7 imports to unit-cli, and unit-cli re-imports back to unit-2 (1) and unit (1), closing a cycle through a third party — the most structurally fragile pattern in this batch
- All five named zones (unit, unit-2/core, unit-cli, analyzers, unit-analyzers) route traffic through sourcevision-analysis-core, making it a mandatory intermediary — any breaking change here has blast radius across the entire package graph
- unit-cli participates in a three-node cycle: unit-2 → unit-cli (7 imports) and unit-cli → unit-2 (1 import) closes a direct cycle, while unit-cli → unit (1 import) and unit → unit-2 (8 imports) closes a second indirect cycle through unit — two overlapping cycles sharing one edge
- sourcevision-analysis-core is the sole mandatory intermediary for all cross-zone traffic in the package — zero zones communicate with each other without passing through it, making it a single point of architectural failure for any zone-boundary refactor
- At 87 files and cohesion 0.9, this zone functionally IS the package — zone detection has produced a near-trivial partition where one zone contains 60% of all files with near-perfect internal cohesion, rendering the zone boundary semantically meaningless for architectural analysis
- The zone name 'sourcevision-analysis-core' is tautological (core of the sourcevision analysis package) — it describes the container, not a functional domain, making zone-level discussions indistinguishable from package-level discussions
- The 7 sub-zones mentioned as internal subdivisions are doing more architectural work than the top-level zone boundary itself — they should be promoted to top-level zones to make the partition actionable
- Zone encompasses 87 files at cohesion 0.9, effectively absorbing the entire package into a single unit. Zone detection has failed to partition the codebase into actionable boundaries — the 7 internal sub-zones should be elevated to top-level zones so that cross-zone import analysis reflects real architectural intent rather than a single monolithic blob vs. a few satellite micro-zones.
- The 'analyzers' sub-zone (8 files, cohesion 0.21) has the lowest cohesion of any named zone, indicating its 8 files do not form a natural cluster. Files grouped there likely share a directory path rather than import relationships — cohesion below 0.3 in an 8-file zone suggests artificial grouping that obscures actual dependencies.
- unit-2 imports from both data-file-schema (foundation layer, correct) AND unit-cli (leaf/CLI layer, incorrect) — a barbell import profile that makes this zone structurally ambiguous: it simultaneously behaves as a domain layer consumer and a CLI layer consumer, which prevents assigning it a clear tier position in any enforced layering contract
- The zone absorbs 76% of all cross-zone traffic yet has no dedicated public API barrel or index.ts that would make its external surface explicit — consumers import arbitrary internal files, meaning the zone boundary exists only in community-detection metadata, not in code structure
- At 87 files and cohesion 0.9, this zone is internally well-structured but externally ungoverned — the high internal cohesion actively masks the severity of its external coupling problems by making the interior feel clean while the boundary remains porous
- Zone imports from unit-cli (7 imports: domain consuming leaf/CLI layer) while also correctly importing from data-file-schema (foundation). This barbell profile — consuming both the foundation layer and the leaf layer simultaneously — means the zone has no stable tier position and cannot be isolated for independent versioning or replacement.
- No index.ts or public barrel file exists at the zone boundary to make the external API surface explicit. All 87 files are directly importable by other zones, meaning zone membership is enforced only by community-detection metadata, not by code structure. Any new import added to any of the 87 files silently extends the zone boundary.
- [call graph] 3203 internal calls, 28 outgoing, 102 incoming (cohesion: 0.99, coupling: 0.01)

</insights>

<sub-zones>

This zone has 7 sub-zone(s):

- **Unit 2/analyzers** (`unit-2/analyzers`): 65 files, cohesion 0.92, coupling 0.08
  - Has 6 nested sub-zone(s)
- **Unit 2/cli** (`unit-2/cli`): 13 files, cohesion 0.64, coupling 0.36
